panels.description: prerender_html jobs waiting for a worker. Reconciles with `SELECT count(*) FROM jobs WHERE status='unfulfilled' AND job_type = 'prerender_html'` minus those with an active reservation.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS pending
  FROM jobs j
  LEFT JOIN job_reservations jr ON j.id = jr.job_id
    AND jr.completed_at IS NULL AND jr.locked_until > NOW()
 WHERE j.status = 'unfulfilled'
   AND j.job_type = 'prerender_html'
   AND jr.id IS NULL;

panels.title: Pending Prerender Jobs

panels.description: prerender_html jobs currently held by a worker (live reservation, finished_at NULL).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS in_flight
  FROM jobs j
  JOIN job_reservations jr ON j.id = jr.job_id
    AND jr.completed_at IS NULL AND jr.locked_until > NOW()
 WHERE j.finished_at IS NULL
   AND j.job_type = 'prerender_html';

panels.title: In-flight Prerender Jobs

panels.description: Age of the oldest pending prerender_html job. Red after 5 minutes. System-tier HTML jobs run at priority 0 and are only claimed by workers whose priority floor is 0, so sustained values here usually mean that pool is saturated or stuck.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (NOW() - MIN(j.created_at))) AS oldest_pending_seconds
  FROM jobs j
  LEFT JOIN job_reservations jr ON j.id = jr.job_id
    AND jr.completed_at IS NULL AND jr.locked_until > NOW()
 WHERE j.status = 'unfulfilled'
   AND j.job_type = 'prerender_html'
   AND jr.id IS NULL;

panels.title: Oldest Pending

panels.description: prerender_html jobs that resolved in the dashboard's time range.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS completed
  FROM jobs
 WHERE job_type = 'prerender_html'
   AND status = 'resolved'
   AND $__timeFilter(finished_at);

panels.title: Completed (range)

panels.description: prerender_html jobs rejected (failed all retries) in the dashboard's time range. A rejected job leaves the affected cards serving their previous HTML; a later index pass for the realm re-publishes the work.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS failed
  FROM jobs
 WHERE job_type = 'prerender_html'
   AND status = 'rejected'
   AND $__timeFilter(finished_at);

panels.title: Failed (range)

panels.description: prerender_html jobs claimed more than once in the dashboard's time range — a lease expired or a worker died mid-run, and the queue re-issued the job.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS retried
  FROM (
    SELECT j.id
      FROM jobs j
      JOIN job_reservations jr ON jr.job_id = j.id
     WHERE j.job_type = 'prerender_html'
       AND $__timeFilter(jr.created_at)
     GROUP BY j.id
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
  ) multi;

panels.title: Retried Jobs (range)

panels.description: Publishes merged into an already-pending prerender_html job for the same realm (per-URL update-wins merge), summed from the surviving jobs' `coalescedPublishes` arg over jobs created in the dashboard's time range. Piggybacks on an in-flight job aren't counted — a running worker holds its args in memory, so the queue records nothing for them.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COALESCE(SUM((j.args->>'coalescedPublishes')::int), 0) AS coalesced
  FROM jobs j
 WHERE j.job_type = 'prerender_html'
   AND $__timeFilter(j.created_at);

panels.title: Publishes Coalesced (range)

panels.description: prerender_html job flow rates. Three series — `arrived` (jobs queued), `started` (a worker reserved them), `completed` (jobs.finished_at set). Bucketed by 30s. A publish that coalesces into a pending job updates it in place rather than creating a new row, so merged publishes don't show as arrivals — see 'Publishes Coalesced'. A persistent gap between arrived and completed indicates worker saturation.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(j.created_at, '30s'),
       COUNT(*) AS arrived
  FROM jobs j
 WHERE j.job_type = 'prerender_html'
   AND $__timeFilter(j.created_at)
 GROUP BY 1
 ORDER BY 1;

SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(jr.created_at, '30s'),
       COUNT(*) AS started
  FROM job_reservations jr
  JOIN jobs j ON j.id = jr.job_id
 WHERE j.job_type = 'prerender_html'
   AND $__timeFilter(jr.created_at)
 GROUP BY 1
 ORDER BY 1;

SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(j.finished_at, '30s'),
       COUNT(*) AS completed
  FROM jobs j
 WHERE j.job_type = 'prerender_html'
   AND j.finished_at IS NOT NULL
   AND $__timeFilter(j.finished_at)
 GROUP BY 1
 ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Prerender throughput (arrived / started / completed)

panels.description: Stocks of prerender_html jobs over time. `pending` = queued, no live reservation; `in_flight` = a worker has an open reservation; `completed` = cumulative finished. Bucketed at 1 minute over the panel's time range. Cost: O(buckets × prerender-jobs in window) per refresh — if this gets slow, switch to a snapshot/materialized table.

targets.rawSql:
WITH buckets AS (
  SELECT generate_series($__timeFrom()::timestamptz, $__timeTo()::timestamptz, '1 minute') AS bucket
),
prerender_jobs AS (
  SELECT j.id, j.created_at, j.finished_at,
    (SELECT MIN(jr.created_at) FROM job_reservations jr WHERE jr.job_id = j.id) AS first_started_at
   FROM jobs j
   WHERE j.job_type = 'prerender_html'
     AND j.created_at <= $__timeTo()::timestamptz
)
SELECT b.bucket AS time,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE pj.created_at <= b.bucket
    AND (pj.first_started_at IS NULL OR pj.first_started_at > b.bucket)
    AND (pj.finished_at IS NULL OR pj.finished_at > b.bucket)) AS pending,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE pj.first_started_at IS NOT NULL
    AND pj.first_started_at <= b.bucket
    AND (pj.finished_at IS NULL OR pj.finished_at > b.bucket)) AS in_flight,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE pj.finished_at IS NOT NULL AND pj.finished_at <= b.bucket) AS completed
 FROM buckets b LEFT JOIN prerender_jobs pj ON TRUE
 GROUP BY b.bucket
 ORDER BY b.bucket;

panels.title: Pending vs in-flight vs completed (over time)

panels.description: One row per prerender_html job currently held by a worker. `progress` and file counts come from `job_progress`, populated by the same event-sink write-through the indexing jobs use. Click the realm cell for the live activity feed; `spawned_by` pivots to the Indexing dashboard focused on the index job whose invalidation set this job renders.

value.title: Delete reservation ${__value.raw}
value.confirmation: Cancel running reservation ${__value.raw}? The worker will stop processing it.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  job_id,
  realm,
  realm_url,
  generation,
  spawned_by,
  files_completed,
  total_files,
  percent,
  percent AS pct,
  elapsed_seconds,
  started_at,
  worker_id,
  reservation_id
FROM (
  SELECT
    j.id AS job_id,
    COALESCE(
      NULLIF(RTRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(COALESCE(j.args->>'realmURL',''), '^https?://[^/]+/', ''), '/'), ''),
      REGEXP_REPLACE(COALESCE(j.args->>'realmURL',''), '^https?://([^./:]+).*$', '\1')
    ) AS realm,
    COALESCE(j.args->>'realmURL','') AS realm_url,
    (j.args->>'generation')::bigint AS generation,
    (j.args->>'spawningJobId')::bigint AS spawned_by,
    COALESCE(jp.files_completed, 0) AS files_completed,
    COALESCE(jp.total_files, 0) AS total_files,
    CASE WHEN COALESCE(jp.total_files, 0) > 0
      THEN (jp.files_completed::float / jp.total_files) * 100
      ELSE 0
    END AS percent,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (NOW() - jr.created_at)) AS elapsed_seconds,
    jr.created_at AS started_at,
    jr.worker_id,
    jr.id AS reservation_id
  FROM jobs j
  JOIN job_reservations jr ON jr.job_id = j.id
    AND jr.completed_at IS NULL AND jr.locked_until > NOW()
  LEFT JOIN job_progress jp ON jp.job_id = j.id
  WHERE j.job_type = 'prerender_html'
    AND j.finished_at IS NULL
    AND (${spawning_job_id:sqlstring} = '' OR j.args->>'spawningJobId' = ${spawning_job_id:sqlstring})
) active
ORDER BY started_at DESC;

panels.title: Active Prerendering

panels.description: Per-realm aggregate of prerender_html job state. `oldest_pending_seconds` red after 5 min flags realms whose HTML backlog isn't draining.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(RTRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(COALESCE(j.args->>'realmURL',''), '^https?://[^/]+/', ''), '/'), ''),
    REGEXP_REPLACE(COALESCE(j.args->>'realmURL',''), '^https?://([^./:]+).*$', '\1')
  ) AS realm,
  COALESCE(j.args->>'realmURL','') AS realm_url,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE j.status = 'unfulfilled' AND jr.id IS NULL) AS pending,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE j.status = 'unfulfilled' AND jr.id IS NOT NULL) AS in_flight,
  MAX(j.finished_at) AS last_completed_at,
  EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (NOW() - MIN(j.created_at)
    FILTER (WHERE j.status = 'unfulfilled' AND jr.id IS NULL))) AS oldest_pending_seconds
 FROM jobs j
 LEFT JOIN job_reservations jr ON j.id = jr.job_id
   AND jr.completed_at IS NULL AND jr.locked_until > NOW()
 WHERE j.job_type = 'prerender_html'
 GROUP BY j.args->>'realmURL'
 ORDER BY pending DESC, in_flight DESC, last_completed_at DESC NULLS LAST
 LIMIT 200;

panels.title: Per-realm prerender status

panels.description: prerender_html jobs waiting on a worker. `urls` is the size of the job's merged change set; `coalesced` counts publishes folded into the job while it waited; `spawned_by` is the index job whose publish created it (the newest publish wins when jobs merge).

value.title: Delete job ${__value.raw}
value.confirmation: Delete waiting job ${__value.raw}? This marks it as completed without running it.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  j.id,
  j.priority,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(RTRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(COALESCE(j.args->>'realmURL',''), '^https?://[^/]+/', ''), '/'), ''),
    REGEXP_REPLACE(COALESCE(j.args->>'realmURL',''), '^https?://([^./:]+).*$', '\1')
  ) AS realm,
  j.status AS status,
  j.created_at AS created_at,
  EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (NOW() - j.created_at)) AS wait_seconds,
  (j.args->>'generation')::bigint AS generation,
  (j.args->>'spawningJobId')::bigint AS spawned_by,
  jsonb_array_length(COALESCE(j.args->'changes', '[]'::jsonb)) AS urls,
  COALESCE((j.args->>'coalescedPublishes')::int, 0) AS coalesced,
  j.id AS job_id
FROM jobs j
LEFT JOIN job_reservations jr ON j.id = jr.job_id
  AND jr.completed_at IS NULL AND jr.locked_until > NOW()
WHERE jr.id IS NULL
  AND j.status = 'unfulfilled'
  AND j.job_type = 'prerender_html'
  AND (${spawning_job_id:sqlstring} = '' OR j.args->>'spawningJobId' = ${spawning_job_id:sqlstring})
ORDER BY j.created_at ASC
LIMIT 500;

panels.title: Queued Prerender Jobs

panels.description: prerender_html jobs that finished in the past 24 hours, newest first. `wait_seconds` is queue time (created_at → first reservation). `run_seconds` is the duration of the attempt that completed it (latest reservation → finished_at). `generation` is the realm index generation stamped on the HTML rows the job wrote; `spawned_by` pivots to the originating index job. Force-cancelled jobs that never got a reservation appear with NULL started_at / wait_seconds / run_seconds / worker_id.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  j.id,
  lr.reservation_id,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(RTRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(COALESCE(j.args->>'realmURL',''), '^https?://[^/]+/', ''), '/'), ''),
    REGEXP_REPLACE(COALESCE(j.args->>'realmURL',''), '^https?://([^./:]+).*$', '\1')
  ) AS realm,
  j.status,
  j.created_at,
  lr.started_at,
  j.finished_at,
  EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (fr.first_started_at - j.created_at)) AS wait_seconds,
  EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (j.finished_at - lr.started_at)) AS run_seconds,
  COALESCE((j.result->>'generation')::bigint, (j.args->>'generation')::bigint) AS generation,
  (j.args->>'spawningJobId')::bigint AS spawned_by,
  jsonb_array_length(COALESCE(j.args->'changes', '[]'::jsonb)) AS urls,
  COALESCE((j.args->>'coalescedPublishes')::int, 0) AS coalesced,
  lr.worker_id
FROM jobs j
LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
  SELECT jr.id AS reservation_id, jr.created_at AS started_at, jr.worker_id
  FROM job_reservations jr
  WHERE jr.job_id = j.id
  ORDER BY jr.created_at DESC
  LIMIT 1
) lr ON TRUE
LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
  SELECT MIN(jr.created_at) AS first_started_at
  FROM job_reservations jr
  WHERE jr.job_id = j.id
) fr ON TRUE
WHERE j.job_type = 'prerender_html'
  AND j.finished_at IS NOT NULL
  AND j.finished_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'
  AND (${spawning_job_id:sqlstring} = '' OR j.args->>'spawningJobId' = ${spawning_job_id:sqlstring})
ORDER BY j.finished_at DESC
LIMIT 500;

panels.title: Completed Prerender Jobs

panels.description: Top 10 user-initiated prerender_html jobs (priority 9 — spawned by user-initiated indexing) that finished in the past 24 hours, ranked by run duration. `run_seconds` measures from the final reservation's `created_at` to `finished_at`, so a job that was retried is timed on the attempt that completed it. These render ahead of system work; regressions here delay HTML behind user edits.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  j.id,
  lr.reservation_id,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(RTRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(COALESCE(j.args->>'realmURL',''), '^https?://[^/]+/', ''), '/'), ''),
    REGEXP_REPLACE(COALESCE(j.args->>'realmURL',''), '^https?://([^./:]+).*$', '\1')
  ) AS realm,
  j.status,
  lr.started_at,
  j.finished_at,
  EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (j.finished_at - lr.started_at)) AS run_seconds,
  jsonb_array_length(COALESCE(j.args->'changes', '[]'::jsonb)) AS urls,
  lr.worker_id
FROM jobs j
JOIN LATERAL (
  SELECT jr.id AS reservation_id, jr.created_at AS started_at, jr.worker_id
  FROM job_reservations jr
  WHERE jr.job_id = j.id
  ORDER BY jr.created_at DESC
  LIMIT 1
) lr ON TRUE
WHERE j.job_type = 'prerender_html'
  AND j.priority >= 9
  AND j.finished_at IS NOT NULL
  AND j.finished_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'
ORDER BY run_seconds DESC NULLS LAST
LIMIT 10;

panels.title: Longest user-initiated prerender jobs (24h)

panels.description: Top 10 system-initiated prerender_html jobs (priority 0 — spawned by boot, deploy, or other system indexing) that finished in the past 24 hours, ranked by run duration. `run_seconds` measures from the final reservation's `created_at` to `finished_at`, so a job that was retried is timed on the attempt that completed it. Use this to spot realms whose full re-render is regressing.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  j.id,
  lr.reservation_id,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(RTRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(COALESCE(j.args->>'realmURL',''), '^https?://[^/]+/', ''), '/'), ''),
    REGEXP_REPLACE(COALESCE(j.args->>'realmURL',''), '^https?://([^./:]+).*$', '\1')
  ) AS realm,
  j.status,
  lr.started_at,
  j.finished_at,
  EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (j.finished_at - lr.started_at)) AS run_seconds,
  jsonb_array_length(COALESCE(j.args->'changes', '[]'::jsonb)) AS urls,
  lr.worker_id
FROM jobs j
JOIN LATERAL (
  SELECT jr.id AS reservation_id, jr.created_at AS started_at, jr.worker_id
  FROM job_reservations jr
  WHERE jr.job_id = j.id
  ORDER BY jr.created_at DESC
  LIMIT 1
) lr ON TRUE
WHERE j.job_type = 'prerender_html'
  AND j.priority < 9
  AND j.finished_at IS NOT NULL
  AND j.finished_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'
ORDER BY run_seconds DESC NULLS LAST
LIMIT 10;

panels.title: Longest system-initiated prerender jobs (24h)

list.description: Filter the job tables to prerender_html jobs spawned by this index job id. Prefilled by the 'View spawned prerender job' drill-through on the Indexing dashboard. Empty = all jobs. When publishes merge, the surviving job carries the newest publish's id, so an older merged-away publish may match nothing — clear the filter to see the realm's jobs.
list.label: Spawning index job
list.name: spawning_job_id
